ecosystem.title: Technology built inside a product and software ecosystem.
ecosystem.text: Hemia.dev is part of Hemia, a product, software and AI company that designs and develops digital solutions for organizations in Mexico and Latin America.

openSourcePage.title: We build in the open.

texts: Turns any repo into a queryable graph (symbols, calls, dependencies) exposed over MCP: agents navigate code structure with hundreds of tokens instead of tens of thousands.

The agentic document framework: define templates as code and generate PDF, DOCX and HTML deterministically. Agents write JSON over MCP; Docrail writes the document with governance, versioning and branding.
The heart of validation at Hemia: define the schema once, get inferred types and readable error messages on client and server.
Accessible components on top of Radix, styled with Hemia's design tokens. Copy the code: no runtime dependency.
Scaffolding, code generators and preview deploys without leaving the terminal.

openSourcePage.text: Libraries, components, templates and tools we use to build products inside the Hemia ecosystem. Open source with intent: every project with a purpose, license and maintainer.
repoDescriptions: Typed data validation, shared between frontend and backend.
Accessible components with custom tokens on top of Tailwind.
Shared TypeScript, ESLint and Prettier configurations.
React hooks we reuse across every product.
Documentation theme built on top of fumadocs.

contribute.title: Contribute.
contribute.text: Issues, ideas and pull requests are welcome in every repository.

standardsPage.title: How we build.
standardsPage.text: Standards and principles we use to make product, design and engineering decisions. They are not universal rules: they are criteria the team maintains and evolves.

principles.title: Code is always reviewed
principles.text: No change reaches main without a second pair of eyes.

principles.title: Boring over clever
principles.text: We pick the solution anyone on the team can maintain at 3am.

principles.title: The problem before the solution
principles.text: Nothing gets built without explaining what problem it solves and for whom.

principles.title: Measure before opining
principles.text: Product decisions are argued with data, not with hierarchy.

principles.title: Tokens, not values
principles.text: No hardcoded colors or radii: everything comes from the design system.

principles.title: Accessible by default
principles.text: Contrast, visible focus and keyboard support are not optional.

principles.title: AI proposes, the team decides
principles.text: Every model output goes through human review before production.

principles.title: Evaluate before shipping
principles.text: Every AI feature has an evaluation set and a quality threshold.

principles.title: Least privilege
principles.text: Every service and person gets only the permissions they need.

principles.title: Secrets out of the code
principles.text: Credentials live in the secrets manager, never in the repository.

principles.title: Every deploy is reversible
principles.text: If it can't be rolled back in minutes, it doesn't ship.

principles.title: Incidents leave lessons
principles.text: Every incident ends in a blameless postmortem.

cta.title: See them in practice.
cta.text: Engineering notes show how these criteria are used — and broken — day to day.
